title: Indie Semiconductor Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text:
Indie Semiconductor reported Q1 2026 revenue of $55.5 million, exceeding guidance by $0.5 million with 3% year-over-year growth. Core business revenue reached $34.1 million, up over 20% sequentially, while Wuxi contributed $21.4 million. The company posted a non-GAAP operating loss of $11.1 million, improving from $15.1 million in the prior-year period. Net loss was $13.9 million, or $0.06 per share on 223 million shares.

Radar Momentum: A Tier 1 customer committed to a $25 million production order for Gen 8 radar technology supporting two major OEMs—one European and one Asian. Management noted: the impending reality of what we're doing here with this huge project is coming to fruition.

Vision Processor Expansion: The iND880 vision processor entered production at NIO for eMirror functionality within six months of design initiation. The DRAMless architecture addresses DRAM supply constraints, with management citing a pipeline of tens of millions of dollars per year in revenue potential, potentially exceeding radar revenue in 2026.

Quantum Computing: Indie introduced the industry's first commercially available 399 nm UV distributed feedback laser for ytterbium cooling in neutral atom quantum systems.

LiDAR Adoption: The iND83301 SoC achieved 80% power reduction and 40% size reduction in FMCW LiDAR systems, expanding into autonomous robotics and embodied AI applications.

Financial Position & Outlook: The company issued $170.5 million in convertible senior notes, using $108 million to repurchase 2027 notes and extending debt maturity by four years. Cash position strengthened to $184.7 million.

Q2 2026 guidance projects $59-$65 million total revenue ($62 million midpoint), with core business contributing approximately $37 million, representing 8% sequential and 20% year-over-year growth. The pending Wuxi divestiture is expected to close later in 2026, generating approximately $135 million in net proceeds.
